fare: Geolocate yourself and discover all the Paris 2024 Games events near you! Celebration venues, cultural events, the torch relay route: live your own Paris 2024 Games experience, with or without a ticket, everywhere in France.
cookiesTxt: En cliquant sur « Accepter tous les cookies », vous acceptez le stockage de cookies sur votre appareil pour améliorer la navigation sur le site, analyser son utilisation et contribuer à nos efforts de marketing.
title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)
titleone: Why did Paris sport open a participatory platform?
txtGlobal: Algerian boxer Imane Khelif qualified for the semi-final at the Paris Olympics after finding herself unwillingly at the heart of a gender controversy, overcame obstacles and prejudices to practice her sport, Saturday, she won hands down hand her quarter-final of less than 66 kg against the Hungarian Luca Anna Hamori, ensuring at least the bronze, which will be the first Algeria's medal at the Paris Games.
txtGlobalI: Kaylia Nemour won the Olympic title on the uneven bars this Sunday, becoming the first gymnast to obtain a medal for Algeria and Africa. Author of an exceptional performance which earned her a score of 15.700, the gymnast of 17 years old struggled to recover from her emotions. “I’m shocked, I don’t realize that I’m an Olympic champion, it’s crazy,” she rejoices at Olympics.com. “For me there is still something after that, it is not the Holy Grail. I'm so proud and happy to have had this medal after all these years of training, after everything I've been through, it's crazy.
txtGlobalII: Despite this image problem, the first Olympic Games organized in France represent an important event, since 997 athletes, including the first 22 women in history, from 24 countries, participated in the 95 events organized
txtGlobalIII: The Olympic character of the events is so little emphasized that many spectators and athletes have, for a long time (and sometimes until their death) no idea that they have attended the Olympic Games.
txtGlobalIIII: the Games are organized again in Paris and in the surrounding municipalities, since 1900, the event has gained momentum. This time, the Games do exist in their own name, and are generating great enthusiasm in the capital. In 1924, 17 sports appeared on the Olympic program for 126 events, during which 3089 athletes competed, including 135 women. The growing scale of the event reached a global scope, since 44 countries sent athletes to the Games, from five continents.
txtGlobalIIIV: A spectacular pre-race lightshow and dramatic music during a lengthy wait for the starting pistol at an expectant Stade de France heightened the senses.But even those dazzling theatrics could not quite do justice to the events which unfolded in the 10 seconds that followed.As Noah Lyles celebrated wildly, his first Olympic triumph confirmed, others were left stunned after witnessing one of the most remarkable 100m showdowns of all time.
